title: Why IREN is Shifting Away from Bitcoin Mining to Data Centers
text:
Iris Energy, rebranded as IREN Ltd. (NASDAQ: IREN), is transforming from a Bitcoin mining company into an AI infrastructure provider. The Sydney-based firm has grown its GPU fleet to 23,000 Nvidia chips with targets to reach $500 million in annualized revenue by late 2025.

The company reported FY25 revenue of $501 million—a 168% year-over-year increase—with net income reaching $86.9 million. Management is constructing a 75MW liquid-cooled facility in Sweetwater, Texas, designed for hyperscale AI workloads.

AI cloud hosting generates 2–3 times more revenue per megawatt compared to mining alone. The strategy sidesteps Bitcoin's halving problem, where rewards diminish every four years.

Wall Street Reception: Analyst sentiment is mixed but predominantly bullish. Roth Capital/MKM set price targets at $82, while J.P. Morgan issued the only Sell rating with a $24 target. The stock has surged over 500% this year.

Risk Factors: GPU shortages, regulatory constraints, and competition from established data center operators like Equinix pose challenges to scaling operations.
